mainEntity.name: How do I know if my site is even eligible to be pulled into Google’s AI overviews?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Google has not published a formal eligibility checklist, but in practice AI overviews favor pages that are crawlable, clearly structured, and show strong expertise and topical depth.

Instead of a yes/no qualification, think in terms of how competitive your content is against pages already cited in overviews for your target searches.

Key factors:
- Your site must allow Googlebot and related crawlers and return clean 200 status codes.
- Pages need substantial, original content that covers the topic in depth, not thin summaries.
- E‑E‑A‑T signals (expertise, experience, authority, trust), including bios and references.
- Existing organic traction: pages already ranking in the top 20 are more likely to be pulled.

Audit the AI overviews for your main keywords and list which domains Google is citing; then assess how your pages compare on depth, clarity, and trust. Prioritize upgrading pages that already get impressions and clicks.

A specialist can help you benchmark your eligibility by mapping your content against what currently appears in AI overviews.
acceptedAnswer.description: There’s no formal eligibility toggle; you become “eligible” by having crawlable, indexable pages with deep, well-structured, trustworthy content that already competes in organic search. Pages in positions 5–20 with strong E‑E‑A‑T are your best candidates.
